measurement_1.value: first blood pressure measurement for a given subject
measurement_1.description: The first blood pressure measurement taken from a subject; one of three equally likely orderings is considered.

measurement_2.value: second blood pressure measurement for a given subject
measurement_2.description: The second blood pressure measurement taken from a subject; one of three equally likely orderings is considered.

measurement_3.value: third blood pressure measurement for a given subject
measurement_3.description: The third blood pressure measurement taken from a subject; the measurement whose position relative to the other two determines whether it is discarded.

number_of_measurements.description: Total number of measurements taken per subject. Used in the answer to reason that each measurement is equally likely to be the middle value, giving a probability of 2/3 that the third is not between the other two.
